title: Lisa Simpson This Isnt Your Life

jokes.text: It's Texxon's way of saying, 'Sorry about those million dead pelicans.'
jokes.setup: Corporate sponsor wants to give away toys to kids
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of environmental disaster as motivation
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire delivered with perfect casualness by Krusty - the juxtaposition of dead wildlife and toy giveaways is brilliantly dark

jokes.text: If you could, I'd make Alex Trebek answer some questions.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Maggie can't climb into TV
jokes.punchline: Turning tables on game show host who asks all the questions
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of expectations with good economy - Homer's fantasy is both specific and character-appropriate

jokes.text: No child of mine will go without anything, ever! Except quality health care!
jokes.setup: Homer making grand declaration about providing for his children
jokes.punchline: Immediate exception that undermines the whole statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline structure with biting social commentary - Homer's casual admission of systemic failure is darkly funny

jokes.text: I'm not cheap, baby, I'm embarrassed to be seen with you. Big difference.
jokes.setup: Date calling him cheap
jokes.punchline: His correction makes it worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy line - his pedantic nature makes him worse off, classic character comedy

jokes.text: Ain't no glass.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's protected by glass barrier
jokes.punchline: Attendant reveals there's no protection at all
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual subversion - the economy of the reveal works well

jokes.text: Multiple D'oh!s as Homer keeps buying gas unsuccessfully
jokes.setup: Homer determined to get the right toy
jokes.punchline: Repeated failure with signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer escalation - functional but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, Maggie, but even at your age, I'm sure you understand things can't always go your way, and that's why I can't believe we didn't get that stupid elf!
jokes.setup: Homer giving mature life lesson to baby
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with childish outburst
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Homer's inability to follow his own advice is perfectly executed

jokes.text: That tree used to be a smaller tree.
jokes.setup: Marge noting how neighborhood has changed
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious about how trees work
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd observation delivered deadpan - perfectly captures how people describe change

jokes.text: And that food bank used to be a regular bank. The march of progress.
jokes.setup: Continuing observations about neighborhood changes
jokes.punchline: Calling economic decline 'progress'
jokes.explanation: Dark social commentary with good ironic twist on the phrase 'march of progress'

jokes.text: Well, stop in and say 'hi' to me 'cause I'll still be there, chillin' in my basement bachelor pad.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking about visiting childhood home as adult
jokes.punchline: Bart's pathetic prediction for his future
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that plays into Bart's underachieving trajectory

jokes.text: Be sure to water the flowers on my backyard grave. / As long as I can dig you up and stick you on the front porch every Halloween. / Just don't dress me up like a woman. / We'll see.
jokes.setup: Casual discussion about death and burial
jokes.punchline: Homer's threat to cross-dress Bart's corpse
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully dark escalation with good character beats - the 'We'll see' is perfect Homer

jokes.text: I just kilt this one because it stopped bein' cute!
jokes.setup: Offering visitors something unexpected
jokes.punchline: Disturbing rationale for killing rabbit
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but funny logic delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I discovered that if I sat in the kitchen, I could see if the bathroom was free using the reflection from the mirror off the toaster.
jokes.setup: Marge showing childhood ingenuity
jokes.punchline: Overly complex solution to simple problem
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing character detail but not particularly strong

jokes.text: I want to go read books in the car. / There are no books in the car. / I just want to sit in the car!
jokes.setup: Lisa making excuse to leave awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Caught in lie, admits she just wants to escape
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's discomfort and quick thinking

jokes.text: Over the years, I've sure been tempted to go through this stuff. So I did, many times.
jokes.setup: Seeming restraint about going through belongings
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of complete violation
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with good economy - the casual delivery sells it

jokes.text: I never really had the hair for these.
jokes.setup: Marge looking at old scrunchies
jokes.punchline: Saying she didn't have the hair when she has the biggest hair ever
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual irony - Marge's iconic towering hair makes this line perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Ooh, here's my paper on ancient history.
jokes.setup: Just called her science fair win 'ancient history'
jokes.punchline: Immediately finds actual paper on ancient history
jokes.explanation: Mild callback pun - functional but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: But then you wound up like you.
jokes.setup: Lisa admiring mother's academic achievements
jokes.punchline: Brutal unfiltered honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Devastating in its simplicity - Lisa's academic honesty cuts right to the heart of family dynamics

jokes.text: The drainage around here is a joke. I don't even know why I pay taxes.
jokes.setup: Bus stuck in puddle
jokes.punchline: Otto complaining about taxes he probably doesn't pay
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Otto acting like responsible citizen is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Just when you think I'm out of ideas!
jokes.setup: Bart about to do another prank
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about his endless creativity for chaos
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment that fits Bart's character well

jokes.text: A new bully has risen. / Simpson! Boombaye!
jokes.setup: Bart accidentally defeats Nelson
jokes.punchline: Kids treating him like a conquering hero with boxing chant
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation with good Muhammad Ali reference

jokes.text: Buying those extra trophies from cash-strapped schools really filled out the old case.
jokes.setup: Skinner showing off trophy case
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of exploiting poor schools
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny revelation that perfectly fits Skinner's pathetic character

jokes.text: Lisa, one of the hardest jobs I have is throwing cold water on young children's dreams. Ralph, you're not a kangaroo.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining his educational philosophy
jokes.punchline: Immediate example of crushing a child's innocent fantasy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - Skinner's matter-of-fact cruelty delivered with perfect timing and character specificity

jokes.text: Ralph, you're also not a trophy.
jokes.setup: Previous crushing of Ralph's kangaroo dreams
jokes.punchline: Continuing to destroy more of Ralph's delusions
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the running gag of crushing Ralph's dreams

jokes.text: I don't want to have to yell at you! / You yell at me about everything. / Well I can't yell at anyone else! Teachers have a union. Students have parents. / What about Willie? / I like Willie!
jokes.setup: Chalmers threatening to yell at Skinner
jokes.punchline: Revealing the bureaucratic hierarchy of who can be yelled at
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamics with smart commentary on workplace hierarchy

jokes.text: Hey, if it hadn't been me, it would have been someone or something else. Sooner or later, everyone meets their Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his impact on Marge's academic decline
jokes.punchline: Making himself a universal force of mediocrity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer's self-awareness and acceptance of his role as a force of entropy is both funny and poignant

jokes.text: It may not be a boy. It could be anything: scrapbooking, high-stakes poker, or the Santa Fe lifestyle. Just pick a dead end and chill out till you die.
jokes.setup: Homer expanding on distractions from dreams
jokes.punchline: Nihilistic life advice delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny life philosophy from Homer - the specific examples are well-chosen

jokes.text: We're both in the same reading group. / I think you know how smart I am. / That's an insult to me and the rest of the inchworms.
jokes.setup: Nelson claiming Lisa thinks she's smart
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out they're in the same low-level reading group
jokes.explanation: Good logic-based comeback with nice detail about reading group names

jokes.text: Are you a distraction or a higher calling?
jokes.setup: Lisa getting rid of all distractions
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis about her saxophone
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Lisa's overthinking nature and academic anxiety

jokes.text: How many jazz musicians led long and happy lives? There's, um...
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to justify keeping saxophone
jokes.punchline: Cannot think of any examples
jokes.explanation: Dark truth about jazz musicians delivered through Lisa's research failure

jokes.text: Oh, that's probably because to her, the saxophone is a me, she doesn't want to end up like you.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain Lisa's behavior
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults Marge while trying to be helpful
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer foot-in-mouth moment with good character logic

jokes.text: Oh, that's probably because to her, the saxophone is a me, she doesn't want to end up like you.
jokes.setup: Marge asking why Lisa threw away her saxophone
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest but poorly articulated explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Homer's clumsy honesty. Awkward delivery fits character but hurts economy.

jokes.text: She does not want to... turn out like... you... didn't... not until... diplomatic... trapped... help. (claps twice) Remember nothing!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to dig himself out of insulting Marge
jokes.punchline: Complete verbal breakdown followed by desperate hypnosis attempt
jokes.explanation: Excellent character panic sequence - Homer's complete meltdown is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Well, if you check your purse, I think you'll find... the seven of clubs! / No. / Just a picture of Lisa-- who wants to be nothing like me.
jokes.setup: Homer attempting magic trick as distraction
jokes.punchline: Failed trick leads back to original problem
jokes.explanation: Good structure where the failed magic trick makes everything worse

jokes.text: No. Just a picture of Lisa-- who wants to be nothing like me.
jokes.setup: Homer's failed card trick
jokes.punchline: Marge's discovery reinforces the original painful truth
jokes.explanation: Good structure - failed distraction makes original problem worse. Solid emotional escalation.

jokes.text: What does your note say? / The seven of clubs? / Ta-dah!
jokes.setup: Kids comparing lunch notes from mom
jokes.punchline: Homer's failed magic trick card shows up
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that pays off earlier setup

jokes.text: ♪ Oh, I'll wax the upstairs ♪ ♪ And I'll wax the downstairs ♪ ♪ And I'll get drunk in the li'bry! ♪
jokes.setup: Willie doing janitorial work
jokes.punchline: Scottish drinking song about school maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good Willie character moment with appropriate Scottish flavor

jokes.text: Nay, just keep studyin'. I'll scream this out.
jokes.setup: Willie severely injured from falling
jokes.punchline: Stoic acceptance of screaming through the pain
jokes.explanation: Excellent Willie moment - his tough Scottish stoicism in face of obvious agony is perfectly character-appropriate

jokes.text: And you'll crack like one of those chicken turds rich people eat. / You mean an egg? / I mean you're dead!
jokes.setup: Nelson making threatening comparison
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know what caviar is, gets corrected on basic eggs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Nelson moment - his attempt at sophisticated threat reveals his ignorance while Lisa's correction makes him more threatening

jokes.text: Lord Buddha, I know I'm not supposed to want stuff, but come on!
jokes.setup: Lisa seeing fancy school
jokes.punchline: Asking Buddha to make exception to non-attachment
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment showing her spiritual struggle with material desires

jokes.text: I'll study so hard, I'll make the old me look like Bart! / That would be one weird-looking kid.
jokes.setup: Lisa promising academic dedication
jokes.punchline: Homer interprets it literally as physical transformation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal interpretation with good visual comedy

jokes.text: I hear they just found out there was a World War II.
jokes.setup: Dismissing Springfield Elementary
jokes.punchline: Extreme example of educational inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Sharp educational satire with good economy

jokes.text: That's the 'I ate the piece of wedding cake she's been saving in the freezer ten years' look. / You what?! / Run!
jokes.setup: Homer recognizing Marge's angry expression
jokes.punchline: Accidentally confessing to different transgression
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Homer's overly specific comparison reveals his own guilt in unrelated matter

jokes.text: Ah-ah-ah-ah-ah. Quiet campus. Shh.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about scholarship
jokes.punchline: Immediate enforcement of strict quiet policy
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing the school's oppressive atmosphere

jokes.text: Oh, no one plays. We just discuss the physics of the game. I lead the team in E.R.A.: Extremely Right Answers.
jokes.setup: Inviting Lisa to join softball team
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's purely academic with acronym pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures the school's overly academic approach to everything

jokes.text: Milhouse couldn't beat me up. / Are you sure? He's having a growth spurt.
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing Milhouse as threat
jokes.punchline: Marge's sincere consideration of Milhouse as potential danger
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Marge's protective concern makes Milhouse seem threatening

jokes.text: They're dentures! / That's social worker talk for 'I'm poor'!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to compliment Nelson
jokes.punchline: Every compliment backfires spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Nelson's defensiveness turns every compliment into an insult

jokes.text: Hey, those memories are forever. You really care, man.
jokes.setup: Nelson being complimented on avoiding face during picture day
jokes.punchline: Actually appreciating this specific compliment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Nelson's sudden vulnerability and appreciation is touching and funny

jokes.text: Teachers teach so much better when they're paid in money, not chickens.
jokes.setup: Lisa praising her new school
jokes.punchline: Implying Springfield Elementary pays in livestock
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable exaggeration of public school underfunding

jokes.text: So, by the power invested in me I now pronounce you sweet dreams.
jokes.setup: Homer saying goodnight for Marge
jokes.punchline: Using wedding ceremony language for bedtime
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer moment with mild wordplay - fits his character well

jokes.text: Everyone's parents are in 'the business.' / What business? / I don't know. They won't tell me.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why school is too elitist
jokes.punchline: Mystery business that excludes outsiders
jokes.explanation: Good observation about elite exclusivity with nice mysterious quality

jokes.text: Must've put away two eight-packs by now.
jokes.setup: Nelson and Bart drinking together
jokes.punchline: Measuring juice box consumption like beer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing kids imitating adult behavior

jokes.text: Told you, ain't got no glass. / Did he take any money? / Uh-uh. / Well, he did now.
jokes.setup: Checking on gas station after Homer's visit
jokes.punchline: Attendant taking opportunity to steal
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that gives closure to the gas station story

jokes.text: Told you, ain't got no glass. Did he take any money? Uh-uh. Well, he did now.
jokes.setup: Attendant explaining how Homer got the toy
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft happening during conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation. Efficient economy in final punchline structure.

tmdb_overview: Fearing that she will end up like Marge, Lisa pledges to focus solely on her academics and not get distracted by other things; Bart puts Nelson in his place and unintentionally earns the title of school bully.
